inboxRoot.description: Root of the agent-bridge inbox (per-target subdirs live under `<inboxRoot>/openclaw/<target>/`). Defaults to ~/.agent-bridge/inbox.

inboxDir.description: Legacy alias for `inboxRoot` (pre-2.1.0). Still honoured for upgrade compatibility.

keysDir.description: Directory holding SSH keys for SFTP outbound replies. Defaults to ~/.agent-bridge/keys.

pollIntervalMs.description: Polling interval for inbox watcher. Default: 2000.

machineName.description: Local machine name used as the 'from' field on outbound BridgeMessages. Defaults to hostname.

agentId.description: OpenClaw agent id to target when building session keys. Defaults to 'main'.

peer_id.description: Plugin-level default peer id (e.g. Ethan's Telegram user id '6164541473'). Used as a fallback when a target's own peer_id is missing OR when auto-discovery can't resolve one from the OpenClaw config / allowFrom list. Optional.

additionalReplyChannels.description: Plugin-level default for which user-facing channel(s) the agent should be HINTED about in the [BRIDGE-CONTEXT] block. The plugin does NOT mechanically fan out the reply — it tells the agent which user-facing channels are configured, and the agent calls the matching reply tool(s). Default policy: telegram-bound targets get ['telegram']; headless targets get []. Special string sentinels: 'none'/'silent'/'off' = quiet mode; 'default' = fall through to the next precedence level. `null` and absent both fall through. Per-target overrides win over this plugin-level default. Per-message override is also supported via the BridgeMessage JSON itself.

replyVia.description: DEPRECATED as of openclaw-channel v3.0.0. The plugin no longer interprets `replyVia` — agent-driven routing replaces it. Use `additionalReplyChannels` instead. The plugin emits a single deprecation warning at register-time if this field is present.

relayNotice.description: Telegram-visible receipt/update for inbound Agent Bridge messages before the agent turn runs. Defaults to enabled; set false (or {"enabled": false}) to silence. The notice starts with '[Agent Bridge relay] 🛰️' so Ethan can identify it at a glance.

relayNoticeChannel.description: Optional channel id for relay notices. Defaults to the target's openclaw_channel (normally telegram).

relayNoticePeerId.description: Optional peer/chat id for relay notices. Defaults to the target peer_id.

targets.description: OPTIONAL: Map of target-name -> routing config. If present, acts as an advanced override — each target name becomes an inbox subdir at `<inboxRoot>/openclaw/<name>/` and identifies which Telegram account/session to inject bridge messages into. If absent or empty (the default), the plugin AUTO-DISCOVERS one target per entry in `channels.telegram.accounts` on the OpenClaw global config. Peer id resolution: explicit `targets.<name>.peer_id` -> `channels.agent-bridge.config.peer_id` -> `meta.user_id` on the global config -> first chat id in `channels.telegram.accounts[<name>].allowFrom`.

openclaw_channel.description: Which OpenClaw channel hosts the target session. Typically 'telegram'.

account.description: Account id within the chosen channel (e.g. 'default', 'clawdiboi2').

peer_id.description: Stable peer id of the direct chat to inject into (e.g. Ethan's Telegram user id '6164541473'). Optional when a plugin-level `peer_id` is configured.

agent_id.description: Optional per-target override of the top-level `agentId`.

additionalReplyChannels.description: Per-target override for the user-facing reply hint surfaced to the agent. Same shape + sentinels as the plugin-level field. `null` and absent both fall through to the plugin-level default, then to the policy default (['telegram'] for telegram-bound targets, [] for headless).

replyVia.description: DEPRECATED as of openclaw-channel v3.0.0. Use `additionalReplyChannels` instead. The plugin emits a single deprecation warning if this field is present and otherwise ignores it.

relayNotice.description: Per-target override for Telegram-visible Agent Bridge relay receipts. Defaults to enabled; set false (or {"enabled": false}) to silence.

relayNoticeChannel.description: Optional channel id for this target's relay notices. Defaults to openclaw_channel.

relayNoticePeerId.description: Optional peer/chat id for this target's relay notices. Defaults to peer_id.

fanOutChannel.description: Deprecated (v2.0.x) — prefer registering the target session directly via `targets.<name>`. Kept for legacy compatibility.

agent-bridge.label: Agent Bridge
agent-bridge.description: Surfaces Agent Bridge messages into the running OC agent's session with a [BRIDGE-CONTEXT] block, and lets the agent's tool calls drive replies (bridge_send_message for the implicit bridge leg, telegram_reply / etc. for additional user-facing legs).
